page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr7.12xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor operating at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. With a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, the instance provides 48 dedicated vCPUs, ensuring that compute resources are not shared with other tenants at the processor thread level.

The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 16 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given the dedicated CPU allocation and high memory-to-core ratio, this instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads requiring consistent compute performance without hyperthreading interference. It represents a high-density resource option for enterprise databases, in-memory caching, and memory-heavy backend applications. The lack of local storage requires external storage provisioning, which should be factored into overall architecture planning.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr7.12xlarge is an x86_64 server instance running on the KVM hypervisor, featuring 48 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor clocked at 3.3 GHz. With a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, it delivers 48 dedicated vCPUs without hyperthreading. The hardware profile includes 384.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per core memory-to-CPU ratio. It contains no local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage options, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps. This memory-optimized configuration is designed for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity and dedicated compute resources, such as in-memory databases, large-scale caching layers, and enterprise applications.
